channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Ukraine Launches Largest Drone Attack on Moscow in Over a Year
item.description: At least four people were killed after Ukraine launched its biggest overnight drone attack on the Russian capital in more than a year, striking targets over 300 miles from the border despite dense Russian air defenses.

item.title: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Separatist Region
item.description: Moldovan leaders denounced Russian President Vladimir Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria separatist enclave as a threat, contemplating measures to counteract it.

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern. The rare Bundibugyo strain has no approved vaccine and has killed nearly 90 people.

item.title: Israel to Build Defense Offices in Former UNRWA Jerusalem Compound
item.description: Israel cabinet approved building a defense compound on the site of recently demolished UNRWA premises in East Jerusalem. The UN had called the demolition a violation of international law.

item.title: Republicans Vie to Replace Defeated Senator Cassidy in Louisiana
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy was defeated in the primary after his impeachment vote against Trump. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming advance to the runoff.

item.title: Air Force Base Locked Down After Idaho Air Show Incident
item.description: Emergency crews responded to an aircraft incident during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho. Witnesses reported mid-air collision involving Navy jets with parachutes seen descending.

item.title: Cuba seeking Russian drones, military equipment as US warns of Guantanamo threat
item.description: US intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay, Key West, and US military vessels. Cuban officials are studying how Iran has resisted US military pressure.

item.title: Ukraine drone strike hits Russian fertilizer plant linked to explosives production
item.description: A fire broke out at Nevinnomyssk Azot plant in Stavropol Krai following a drone strike. The facility is one of Russia's largest mineral fertilizer producers and a critical component of Russia's defense-industrial complex for explosives manufacturing.

item.title: US sanctions waiver for Russian oil purchases expires without renewal
item.description: The Treasury Department allowed the waiver permitting countries to purchase Russian oil cargoes stranded at sea to expire on May 16. Treasury Secretary Bessent had previously said there were no plans to renew the authorization.

item.title: New Epstein victims emerge in French investigation
item.description: Paris prosecutor says around 10 new victims have come forward since France launched a special probe into Jeffrey Epstein's network in February. Investigators are working to interview victims located abroad.

item.title: US Intelligence Warns of Cuban Drone Threat to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Classified intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 military drones and discussed plans to attack the US base at Guantanamo Bay, US naval vessels, and potentially Key West, Florida.

item.title: Taiwan President Rejects Being "Sacrificed," Defends US Arms Sales
item.description: President Lai Ching-te said Taiwan will not be traded away and US arms sales represent a security commitment, responding to Trump comments urging both sides to cool down.

item.title: Israel Economy Contracts in Q1 as Iran War Takes Toll
item.description: Israels economy shrank at an annualized 3.3% in the first quarter, hit by the war with Iran, but growth is expected to recover if the conflict does not reignite.

item.title: China Commits to $17B Annual US Agricultural Purchases Through 2028
item.description: China has agreed to purchase at least $17 billion of US agricultural products annually through 2028, the White House announced, offering stability to American farmers after Phase One trade deal.

item.title: Trump to Hold Situation Room Meeting Tuesday on Iran Military Options
item.description: President Trump is expected to meet with top national security advisers on Tuesday to discuss military options regarding Iran, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled.

item.title: Ukraine Commander Vows Relentless Strikes Into Russia
item.description: Ukrainian unmanned forces commander "Madyar" says Kyiv will continue targeting Russian defense industry and oil facilities across all of Russia, from the Urals to Siberia, to deprive Moscow of war funding.

item.title: IDF Reports Shortage of 12,000 Troops Amid Multi-Front War
item.description: The Israeli military announced a critical manpower gap of approximately 12,000 soldiers, including 6,000-7,500 combat troops, as it operates across seven different arenas.

item.title: Kremlin Interested in Restoring Dialogue with Europe Over Ukraine War
item.description: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Russia is interested in restoring relations with European countries amid the war in Ukraine, following an attack in Moscow that killed two Ukrainian officials.

item.title: Train Driver Charged After Deadly Crash in Thailand
item.description: A train driver was charged with reckless driving causing death after a collision in Thailand that killed at least two people and injured dozens more.

item.title: Islamist Attack on Nigerian Military School Kills 17 Police Trainees
item.description: An Islamist attack on a Nigerian military school killed 17 police trainees in the latest violence in the country's north. Boko Haram or a splinter group is suspected.

item.title: LIRR Workers Authorize Strike, Raising Transit Disruption Fears
item.description: Long Island Rail Road workers voted to authorize a strike, potentially disrupting commuter rail service for millions of New York area passengers.

item.title: Peru Electoral Body Pledges Fix for Voting Flaws Before June Runoff
item.description: Peru's electoral authority pledged to address voting system flaws identified in the first round before the June presidential runoff between Fujimori and Sanchez.

item.title: Drone Strike Sparks Fire at UAE Nuclear Plant, Escalating Regional Tensions
item.description: A drone strike sparked a fire near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the UAE. Authorities confirmed no radiation impact. Gulf states and Egypt condemned the attack amid Iran-US ceasefire tensions.

item.title: Drone Strike on UAE Nuclear Plant Marks Major Escalation in Iran Conflict
item.description: A drone hit the UAE's Barakah Nuclear Power Plant, sparking a fire. The UAE condemned the attack as a threat to regional security. The incident comes as Trump warned Iran that time is running out for a peace deal.
